source: User request on 2026-05-12 accepted via ask_user: Adopt + open task.
summary: Accepted Control Room simplification: second-screen UI should maximize signal-to-noise beside chat, reduce top-level sections, curate product/system data, and focus Board/Graph on roadmap work.

diff_table.current_state: Control Room top-level nav includes Home, System, Graph, Knowledge, Board, Builds, Validation, Diff, and Settings.
diff_table.desired_state: Control Room top-level nav is reduced to Status, Product, System, Board, and Graph; other details move to inspectors, contextual links, API/chat flows, or hidden utility surfaces.
diff_table.rationale: The UI is a second screen used beside agent chat, so high-signal overview and navigation matter more than exposing every artifact as a section.

diff_table.current_state: Home acts as a mission briefing with many factors and area previews.
diff_table.desired_state: Home is renamed Status and limited to basic project metrics/statistics, health, focus, and next action.
diff_table.rationale: Status should answer what is happening quickly without duplicating chat or raw graph/task output.

diff_table.current_state: Product docs are mostly Markdown sources, and UI expectations allow browsing source-backed areas directly.
diff_table.desired_state: Product UI curates users, stories, and UI information from product Markdown into useful cards/relationships; raw Markdown remains available as source, not primary UX.
diff_table.rationale: Markdown alone is not a good user experience for product understanding.

diff_table.current_state: System diagram source is a single architecture.mmd at kb/system root, and system docs require flat component docs.
diff_table.desired_state: System keeps component Markdown docs at kb/system/*.md and adds kb/system/diagrams/** for agent-editable raw diagram data selected by the UI.
diff_table.rationale: Multiple representative system diagrams need structured raw data that agents can maintain and UI renderers can consume.

diff_table.current_state: No accepted core set or canonical raw formats exists for the most important project diagrams.
diff_table.desired_state: Use five core diagrams: context map, component/container map, key flow sequence, data/domain model, and state/lifecycle map. Store canonical diagram specs as YAML, with renderers generating Mermaid/Cytoscape/custom SVG views as needed.
diff_table.rationale: YAML is easier for agents to edit safely than dense diagram DSL while still allowing deterministic rendering.

diff_table.current_state: Board/Graph already expose roadmap/graph state but also compete with Diff/Builds/Validation sections.
diff_table.desired_state: Board and Graph stay top-level but focus on roadmap work mapping, closure evidence, active tasks/sprints, and graph relationships around work; build/validation/diff details appear contextually.
diff_table.rationale: Roadmap work is what users need to track visually; detailed compiler artifacts are better handled in chat or inspectors.

accepted_decisions.summary: Create a new follow-up task instead of mutating archived completed UI tasks.
Treat the Control Room as a high-signal second screen used alongside chat.
Deprecate top-level Knowledge, Builds, Validation, Diff, and Settings sections while preserving source-backed access through inspectors/contextual links/chat.
Use agent-editable YAML specs under .codewiki/kb/system/diagrams/** as canonical raw diagram data.

assumptions: Archived roadmap tasks remain historical truth; new work opens as a follow-up task.
The initial diagram YAML format can be refined during implementation if renderer constraints require minor schema changes.
non_goals: Do not implement UI code in the documentation step.
Do not remove underlying CodeWiki capabilities for builds, validation, feedback diff tables, or settings.
Do not make UI-only state canonical truth.

roadmap: new Control Room simplification follow-up task
